title: `IRedistList<T>.GetEnumerator()` should use `db.ListRange()` instead of `db.ListGetByIndex()`
author: occluder
body:
As the title says, RedisList should be using the ListRange method, as it is many times faster and cheaper to query. I ran the following benchmarks, to retrieve 10, 100 and 1000 items from a list to compare performance:

![image](https://github.com/thepirat000/CachingFramework.Redis/assets/69414142/09a87b42-4d8e-4672-a7bd-6f52394cba4e)

title: BinarySerializer isn't available on .NET 6.0
author: du8die
body:
I'm trying to get the framework running on a .NET 6.0 project.

RedisContext.DefaultSerializer = new BinarySerializer(); leads to a "The type or namespace name 'BinarySerializer' does not exist in the namespace 'CachingFramework.Redis.Serializers' (are you missing an assembly reference?)"

When I use intellisense, the Serializers object has RawSerializer and JsonSerializer.

Any idea what I'm doing wrong?

Thanks,

title: Update CachingFramework.Redis.MsgPack to support DateOnly and TimeOnly serialization
author: wrkntwrkn
body:
`System.DateOnly is not registered in resolver: MessagePack.Resolvers.StandardResolver
   at MessagePack.FormatterResolverExtensions.Throw`

As the exception says, `DateOnly` cannot be serialized/deseralized. According to the [following issue](https://github.com/neuecc/MessagePack-CSharp/issues/1240), this is now supported.

My contract example:
`[MessagePackObject] public sealed record ContractExample([property: Key(0)] DateOnly SomeDate);`

Current version:
`<PackageReference Include="CachingFramework.Redis.MsgPack" Version="15.0.1" />`

title: Nuget package is unoptimized
author: ovska
body:
The `CachingFramework.Redis 14.1.0`-package is unoptimized, as reported by [UnoptimizedAssemblyDetector](https://www.nuget.org/packages/UnoptimizedAssemblyDetector):

> UnoptimizedAssemblyDetector.targets(14, 5): Unoptimized assembly detected: 'CachingFramework.Redis.dll' at /home/xxx/.nuget/packages/cachingframework.redis/14.1.0/lib/net6.0/CachingFramework.Redis.dll

Release-configuration should probably be used in `dotnet pack`-commands in `pack.cmd`. [The default is Debug](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/core/tools/dotnet-pack#options:~:text=The%20default%20for%20most%20projects%20is%20Debug).

title: Clear all keys + clear the entire database
author: Misiu

title: Bulk Insert Question
author: Marty-Jones
body:
Do you have any suggestions on how I could use a "Batch" to bulk insert items that include tags?
I have a case where I need to bulk insert 50-100 items at one time.  Each of these items have associated tags with them.

FYI, I will not be running under a Redis cluster for this.

title: Tag Prefix Feature
author: Marty-Jones
body:
I would like to request the ability to specify a custom tax prefix instead of the hardcoded value ":$_tag_$:{0}".   

There are situations where we have application data that is prefixed and we would like the tags associated with that data to be prefixed as well.   Basically allow the user to specify the tag prefix "{0}:{1}" so 0 would be what the override value is or default to ":$_tag_$".

title: Getting the keys of a dictionary fail using System.Text.Json
author: emorell96
body:
When you get the keys of a dictionary using .Keys it fails if the type of the keys are string.

This happens I believe because the serializer serializes a string like `"my string"` (a string with double quotes), but then when you pass this to Redis, it strips the outter double quotes. Then when you get the keys directly from Redis you get this:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20151415/168087640-3709824a-093e-4f88-9029-206be6f68c69.png)

Then of course the library selects `Serialize(key)` which is already serialized (without the double quotes), and it fails.

This line:

https://github.com/thepirat000/CachingFramework.Redis/blob/081984b70a56c6b8164a09b44642a9c22ee5e060/src/CachingFramework.Redis/RedisObjects/RedisDictionary.cs#L251

I am investigating and will push to the open PR.

title: How to define timeout in FetchObject ?
author: sayedgt
body: **context.Cache.FetchObject** allows to fetch items from cache and if no data found it returns data from service call which is brilliant. It also updates the cache. But how to set the timeout here?

title: Tags TTL is not updated when KeyExpire is used
author: lucamnn
body:
When a call to KeyExpire is made, tags entries are not consequently updated. They stuck on initial TTL from the key .
So it happens that the keys are still active, but they lose the related tags.

title: Timeout on small list
author: attrib75

title: Add a  Method AddRange with additional parameter Tags to RedisDictionary
author: saarfa
body:
I use Redis on top of SQLSever, every table has a corresponding hash.
I use Tags as foreign keys to create tables relations.
In order To support bulk insert of rows having the same parent table relation i need
void AddRange(IEnumerable<KeyValuePair<TK, TV>> items, string[] tags);
in  RedisDictionary.
Doe's creating one will be incompatible with a cluster topology.
